description: Create a content slide on business considerations for private credit exposure using a three-column layout with industries, finance actions, and operations actions in bullet lists.

header.headline: Private credit now spans a broad set of industries, from manufacturing and TMT to cleantech and life sciences, so stress in the asset class carries economy-wide implications

items.text: Sectors with high private credit penetration
Industries with growing private credit presence
Stress-test covenants and refinancing under higher for-longer rate scenarios and lower valuation exit multiples
Explore equity/quasi equity (preferred, convertible, minority PE) to de-risk balance sheet where leverage was set off peak AI / tech valuations
Match tenor and structure of private-credit facilities to underlying asset cash flows (e.g., avoid short-dated bullet maturities on long-dated projects)
Monitor build up of private credit facilities alongside traditional bank lines to avoid hidden leverage

items.sub: Faster billing and collections; Smarter inventory and procurement to free up cash

items.text: Protect and grow cash-margin, not just revenue
items.sub: Prioritise high-margin products, contracts and sites; Pause or reshape low-return initiatives

items.text: Prepare cost-flexibility playbooks (e.g., pre-agreed levers for slowing hiring, reducing discretionary spend, and resizing capacity if volumes disappoint)

items.sub: Short, frequent cash, and covenant forecasts; Shared view between finance and operations on headroom and risks
